examples: Trading was important, as so many raw materials, the semiprecious stones for the decoration of the temples, timbers for roofs, and all metals, had to be imported.
A green variety of microcline, often used as a semiprecious stone.
The trophy was made of pure gold with a base of semiprecious stones.
"I'm basically stone-mad, " she said, adding that she'd like to create more furniture and decorative objects with semiprecious gems.
The gems are olivine--a mineral class that includes semiprecious peridot--and can range in color from deep green to bright yellow.
